Earring of Cleansing  
 

Lore Item
Slot: EAR
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 2
WIS: +1 HP: +35 MANA: +35
Effect: Counteract Poison (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 5.0) at Level 49
WT: 0.3 Size: SMALL
Class: CLR SHM
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Earring of Cleansing
Lore Item
Slot: EAR
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 2
WIS: +1 HP: +35 MANA: +35
Effect: Counteract Poison (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 5.0) at Level 49
WT: 0.3 Size: SMALL
Class: CLR SHM
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:0 pp 3 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:780
Submitted By:Miraly Lifecaster
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-14 01:02:08
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 1,650pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 50
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Chardok
NPC Name
An Iksar Trustee


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Chardok


Uploaded January 1st, 2022 by iventheassassin

Post Comment
What lvl does this proc at?
QuoteReply
# Feb 26 2003 at 11:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591783975370
2 posts
I have this item and also the Earring of Purity. Does anyone know what lvl it procs at. And don't just try to guess unless you know, I have it when people try to bullcrap around. If you don't really know then don't reply.

# Feb 27 2003 at 2:45 PM Rating: Excellent

429 posts
49
▲ Page top
When does it proc.
QuoteReply
# Nov 22 2002 at 5:14 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591779140129
View User Forum
206 posts
I've been wearing my Forest Loops for about 15 levels and I just got one of these at 45 and it won't proc. I bought it for the stats (paid 1.5K) but the proc would be nice (so I don't have to cast that last healing spell to counteract the effects of a poison dot).
▲ Page top
Earrings
QuoteReply
# Nov 17 2002 at 5:56 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591776410526
Sage
280 posts
Shaman can wear both the cure poison and cure disease earrings, however I find this one much more usefull for fighting wyverns in SF as my pet gets constantly poisoned, and I dont have to eat up a spell slot for it. The earring that casts spirit of scale is nice to for making long runs shorter, but dont cast while its in effect or you will be running non-sowed. The stats are nice as well. These will be probably the first upgrade a WIS caster sees in the earring dept. unless you quest for a Ivandyrs Hoop, which is very nice earring, even w/o the 6 charges of 150 hp lifetap. I paid like 1800pp each for mine, going rate was 2500pp each at the time. Great deal and the cast time is the same as the spell. Click gear rules.

56 Ogre Luminary on Luclin server--Chaotic Winds--Baron Aggrok
▲ Page top
got earring tonite
QuoteReply
# Sep 27 2002 at 1:48 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255685773
1 post
killed iskar trustee and got this off him, chanter named rainy onluclin server won roll and gave itto me, since i still had forrest loops as a 57 cleric. Very thankful she gave it to me, and i never complained about not getting it. iskar trustee is deeper in mines but not too hard of a kill.
▲ Page top
VERY useful
QuoteReply
# May 18 2002 at 1:24 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591753632248
Scholar
45 posts
I dont know why you would think these are bad earrings. Earrings alone are hard to come by, and usually awarded to tanks for AC, or INT casters. Most of the clerics I know are still wearing crafted, or forest loops.

These are awesome because of the hp/mana. Earrings typicly have very low stats on them, so 35/35 is a high rating. Also click effects are priceless in their own right. These spells are a pain to pull up, because they are so rarely used. It so much nicer to pull down a quick key to use the earring, rather than lose a spell slot.
▲ Page top
WTB
QuoteReply
# Apr 29 2002 at 8:05 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248758846
View User Forum
161 posts
OK ive been looking for this earring forever..Hasnt dropped for me in Chardok yet..I have the Earring of Purity and I love it..Exspecially while hunting in Seb..darn necros hehe..I am willing to pay more then this earring is worth...On Brell they sell for 1kpp I am willing to pay 2kpp..so if you have one on Brell send me a tell in game..Im either on Eberk or my bard Haarmony Dreamdiva..thank you..well good hunting and safe journey to all.
▲ Page top
Nice mana upgrade
QuoteReply
# Nov 30 2001 at 11:38 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248446277
117 posts
Guys once you hit 200 in wis you need to get mana items ..these are Very nice earings to those who have the wis to play with.
▲ Page top
RE: Nice mana upgrade
QuoteReply
# Apr 15 2002 at 3:11 PM Rating: Decent
Azuarc
View User Forum
Guru
6,678 posts
Agreed. I have 242 wisdom now on my cleric, and I'm still wearing boring old 3 wis crafted earrings. At level 60 and below 200 wis, 3 wis is worth 36 mana. Above 200 wis, I dunno.
So instead of 3 wis = 36 mana, I get 1 wis, 35 mana = 47 mana. Plus, I get 35 HP, a couple AC and a right click effect.
What you may not be considering is that this is an EAR slot. There aren't that many attainable ear items that are better than a gold jasper stud. Throw high-end raiding zones out like ToV and SSra, and you're left with a couple drops in Maiden's that I've yet to actually hear of anyone getting, and these in Chardok.
____________________________
Only the exceptions can be exceptional.
▲ Page top
RE: Nice mana upgrade
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2002 at 10:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770853819
24 posts
At level 60 with 200+ wis, a 3wis earring is worth 10-15 mana. These earrings are probably the best droppable cleric earrings.
▲ Page top
RE: Nice mana upgrade
QuoteReply
# Nov 18 2002 at 2:14 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591779140129
View User Forum
206 posts
Here is the Mana formula as I have been led to understand it. 0.2 times your level times your wisdom (up to 200 wisdom) and 6 mana per wisdom above 200. so a 35 mana earring is the equivalent of about 6 wisdom.

My wisdom hit the 255 cap a long time ago so extra wisdom on items has absolutely no draw for me. I look for mana first and foremost then Resists/HP/AC or other stats but Wisdom is useless to me.

Edited, Mon Jun 30 17:46:03 2003
▲ Page top
CHARDOK?
QuoteReply
# Nov 27 2001 at 10:10 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770753025
29 posts
I agree that the effect is very usefull. Hell i would love to have something like that. Alas won't work for a druid. Should be a upper 20s lower 30s quest or something for the stats. Maybe a camp off of some mob, or a drop in a lower to mid level dungeon like Najena or Paw. Do they make a similar version of this item for druids?
▲ Page top
Space saver
QuoteReply
# Nov 17 2001 at 11:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767904358
22 posts
As a cleric this would in my opinion be great for keeping a spell slot open for myself anyway. It would be very handy when soloing. Plus then i don't have to wait for my shining star of light (awesome quest for low levels) which i used until 20. Flame me if you must, but they always put in something in high level zones that, for lack of a better term, just don't make a lot of sense.
▲ Page top
How Useful?
QuoteReply
# Nov 13 2001 at 9:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770849382
19 posts
Okay this item is nice for the fact of having the counteract poison and yes it is a shame that druids aren't allowed to use it but Do you think that this should be attainable to a lower level PC? I mean +1wis, +2 AC and +35HP/MP seems that it would be on a MoB in Najena or a little lower level Dungeon. Just seeems like a lower level item than 50! --my opinion and they're just like A**holes --everyone has one!

#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 12 2001 at 2:02 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) The 'people' that make it a lifestyle to post on this site about a ******* video game are worthless no life having can't get a date living with their parents never heard of a shower immature selfish jobless ****** >>******<< morons that couldn't tell the difference between the outside of their house and their own *******.
RE: The Truth
QuoteReply
# Nov 13 2001 at 11:11 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762542512
4 posts
Um reality check here...... WTF are you doing? You are doing the same thing you acuse other people of doing. What do you actually sit at your computer desk waiting for a response to your flames so you yourself can spend another 5 minutes of your life to write a response?

What gives you the right to decide what someone can and cant do? It obviously troubles you to no end that somebody is posting on these forums but yet you find and irrisitable fascination to do the same.

So Get your head out of your wife's *** take your kids to Mc'donalds and forget you even saw this webpage. Because you obviously enjoy bringing missory to others you self righteous ignorant spore of a baboons *****

Now what would really impress me is if you found the sense of control not to respond to this post. I know its in you somewhere. Seeing as how that it is a waist of prescious time.

So in closing i would like to say please for all that is good I hope you are venting on this board and not on your wife and child. God help their souls. Because if this is the true indication of your actions IRL then you my friend are the the one who really has no life. Dont spread the disease of your hatred. Grab a hammer and take out your (sexual?) frustrations on your Computer. Maybe then you can feel you have a purpose in your pathetic life.
Ps: crack the window, the air is getting musty in your computer room.

Great earring
QuoteReply
# Nov 11 2001 at 10:19 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591767211061
View User Forum
110 posts
Counteract poison and the like aren't spells that are ever memorized. This is a great earring simply for the effect.
▲ Page top
Rangers and Druids
QuoteReply
# Nov 11 2001 at 6:25 PM Rating: Good
Valdon
674 posts
Doesnt it seem lame to give casters and Item like this. I mean I play a cleric, the item looks cool, but I can cure poison. Why no Rangers or Druids... who have a bit weaker healing and curing. OR even like classes that can not cast at all. They are the ones out in the middle of no where, and no cleric around, that need items like this.

Besides it looks like it would be a ranger / Druid Item. All nature loving :)

There is also a toss up there, would you rather have an EARING that raises a stats or possibly even RESIST poison. Or a charge Item?

Kind of a toss up. Good stats... a resist poison would be cool. I till think Rangers and Druids should be able to use this. It would be handy to both.
____________________________
· 'Valdon Gravedancer' Terris-Thule. Dwarven Cleric 34th circle.
· 'Junglebug Sugarpants' Terris-Thule. Halfling Druid. 52nd season.
· 'Shinook Eldergod' Terris-Thule. Troll Shaman of 32nd rite.
· 'Soultree Vixen' Terris-Thule. Woodelf Ranger of 34th season.

# Nov 11 2001 at 9:25 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750928605

Don't feel too bad. There's a very nice "Earring of Twisted Leaves" that's DRU SHM only. Same stats, effect is Scale of Wolf.
▲ Page top
RE: /sigh
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2001 at 2:23 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591751748279
19 posts
Yes, clerics are left off on the Earring of Twisted Leaves. Of course, Scale of Wolf is not a cleric spell. Counteract Poison is castable by druids, however, so making this a cleric/shaman only earring seems a little arbitrary.
▲ Page top
This earring rocks for...
QuoteReply
# Nov 10 2001 at 12:48 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592245555079
17 posts
duels and PVP. Counteract Poison dispells Tashan debuffs, so your MR wouldn't always get screwed by an enchanter.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 09 2001 at 10:13 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Oh wow... unlimited charges of Counteract Poison... on a PvP server this would rule (and presumably for duels on PvP- servers), as anyone who's ever been tashed would know. Sure clerics get cure poison anyhow, but honestly, who keeps that spell memmed? This is a sweet, sweet earring.
Whereabouts
QuoteReply
# Nov 09 2001 at 8:48 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591755853213
Scholar
102 posts
This and the Earring of Purity (exact same, except the triggered effect is Counteract Disease), are both dropped by the Iksar Trustee down in Chardok a tunnel or so away from the Herb House.

Off-hand, I would say this is the rarer of the two items, but in about 7 kills, the iksar has always dropped one of the earrings, (5 disease earrings, 2 poison), so it is not difficult to get a full set.
▲ Page top
Very nice indeed
QuoteReply
# Nov 09 2001 at 8:23 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591760700540
67 posts
I'd love to have this; Currently I'm using an imbued ivory golden earring or whatever, something like 4ac 4wis and 4sv cold, on one ear.. The other has the halloween ear. I would lose 3 wis, and even though with my current stuff on (mostly AC stuff, started with 80 wis though), the extra HP would mean more cannis and the +35 mana more than makes up for lost 3 wis.. And then of course there's the mana-free Counteract Poison =)
Never used the spell itself much, but would be great if you accidentally EBolted/VoTSed yourself and didn't have time to mem the spell =)
▲ Page top
Usefull
QuoteReply
# Nov 09 2001 at 6:19 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591768412570
205 posts
Very nice earrings. Extra mana and hp after you've maxxed Wis. A little AC, and you can cast free Counteract Poison. I personaly can't see these becomming all that popular, but (if a druid could use them) I'd definaly love them.
Too bad you can't wear two. :)

Meirril

p.s. anyone know what dropped this?
